steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Before the blank: "... were completed in 1570 and" — needs a year + "respectively" to finish the first independent clause. After: "such allegorical artwork was particularly popular..." — a second independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined with a semicolon (or period). The list-end adverb "respectively" attaches to the first clause and is set off by commas before the joining punctuation.

That gives: "...1570 and 1712, respectively; such allegorical..." — Choice B — 1712, respectively;.

A omits the second year. C uses a comma between two ICs (comma splice). D mispositions the semicolon (cuts "respectively" from its clause).

That matches Choice B — 1712, respectively;.
